Hero.title: Presidency of the Republic of Moldova of the Council of Europe
Hero.description: The 135th Session of the Committee of Ministers will take place on 14–15 May 2026 at the Palatul Republicii in Chișinău, marking the conclusion of the Moldovan Presidency and the handover to Monaco.

About.title: The 135th Session of the Committee of Ministers
About.intro: The Session will bring together Ministers for Foreign Affairs and other high-ranking representatives of the member States to provide political guidance and strategic direction to the Organization.
About.paragraph1: As the decision-making body of the Council of Europe, the Committee of Ministers is responsible for setting priorities, adopting decisions and recommendations, supervising the execution of judgments of the European Court of Human Rights, and ensuring the effective implementation of the Organisation's standards and commitments across its member States.
About.paragraph2: The meeting will offer an opportunity to assess progress achieved in advancing human rights, democracy and the rule of law, while addressing emerging challenges facing the European continent.
About.highlight: The 135th Session will also mark the conclusion of the Presidency of the Republic of Moldova of the Committee of Ministers. This moment will provide an occasion to reflect on the priorities advanced during the Presidency and to ensure continuity of action, as the Presidency is formally handed over to Monaco.

Participants.title: Participating States
Participants.description: Invitations to attend the 135th Session of the Committee of Ministers of the Council of Europe were extended to the representatives of the 46 member States of the Council of Europe, as well as to the Council of Europe's observer States.
Participants.memberStates: Member States of the Council of Europe

Venue.title: Palatul Republicii
Venue.description: The Palace of the Republic is a building located in Chișinău city centre. Inaugurated in 1984, it has a unique architectural value and is included in international registers which incorporate the most sumptuous buildings in the world.
Venue.address: Maria Cebotari Street 16, Chișinău, Republic of Moldova

Priorities.title: Priorities of the Presidency
Priorities.subtitle: The Republic of Moldova joined the Council of Europe on 13 July 1995. Taking over for a second time the presidency of the Committee of Ministers in the year of its 30th membership anniversary is both highly symbolic and of utmost importance.
Priorities.intro1: In 30 years of Council of Europe membership, our democracy has been consolidated, the rule of law reformed, the protection of human rights improved and extended, and the civic space strengthened. The Council of Europe has been a genuine and trusted partner for the Republic of Moldova.
Priorities.intro2: The new realities in which we are operating make our Organisation even more significant, highlight the need for renewed responsibility and commitment for its core values, and call for resolute actions to strengthen our collective response to the challenges to its fundamental principles.
Priorities.motto: For a Europe of Peace and Resilience

ukraine.title: Support for Ukraine
ukraine.description: The Presidency will facilitate the Council of Europe's support to Ukraine through capacity-building, monitoring and co-operation initiatives and the strengthening of its mechanisms to ensure accountability, including the operationalisation of the Special Tribunal and setting up the Claims Commission.

democracy.title: New Democratic Pact for Europe
democracy.description: The Moldovan Presidency will support the Council of Europe's process towards a New Democratic Pact for Europe. Democracy is a process, not a destination — preserving and defending it requires genuine commitment, consistent actions, and innovation.

disinformation.title: Combating Disinformation
disinformation.description: Strengthening our response to disinformation, including foreign information manipulation and interference, to defend freedom, uphold the rule of law and protect fundamental rights across Europe.

Footer.description: Presidency of the Republic of Moldova of the Committee of Ministers of the Council of Europe. 14–15 May 2026, Chișinău.
Footer.copyright: Presidency of the Republic of Moldova of the Council of Europe. All rights reserved.

hero.title: Discover the Republic of Moldova
hero.subtitle: A land of rolling vineyards, rich traditions, and warm hospitality — your guide to exploring Moldova during the 135th Session.

about.title: About Moldova
about.description: Nestled between Romania and Ukraine, the Republic of Moldova is a hidden gem of Eastern Europe — a country of verdant landscapes, centuries-old winemaking traditions, and a vibrant cultural heritage.

capital.label: Capital
capital.value: Chișinău — a green, walkable city with tree-lined boulevards and a mix of Soviet-era and modern architecture.

population.label: Population
population.value: Approximately 2.6 million people, known for their warmth and hospitality.

language.label: Language
language.value: Romanian (official). Russian and English are widely spoken in Chișinău.

currency.label: Currency
currency.value: Moldovan Leu (MDL). Major credit cards are accepted. ATMs are widely available.

timezone.label: Time Zone
timezone.value: Eastern European Time (EET, UTC+2). Summer time: EEST (UTC+3).

climate.label: Climate in May
climate.value: Warm and pleasant, 18–25°C. Light layers and comfortable shoes are recommended.

airport.name: Chișinău International Airport
airport.description: Chișinău International Airport (KIV) offers direct connections to major European cities including Bucharest, Istanbul, Vienna, Frankfurt, Rome, and Paris.

practical.title: Practical Information
practical.subtitle: Essential details for delegates visiting Chișinău.

visa.title: Visa Information
visa.description: Citizens of Council of Europe member States and observer States can enter Moldova visa-free for stays up to 90 days. A valid passport is required.

electricity.title: Electricity
electricity.description: Type C and F plugs, 230V / 50Hz — same as most European countries. No adapter needed for EU delegates.

internet.title: Connectivity
internet.description: Free Wi-Fi is widely available in hotels, restaurants, and public areas. Local SIM cards with data can be purchased at the airport.

whatToSee.title: Discover & Explore
whatToSee.subtitle: From Chișinău's green parks to underground wine cities — Moldova has more to offer than you might expect.

stefan.title: Ștefan cel Mare Park
stefan.description: The heart of Chișinău — a beautiful central park named after Moldova's greatest medieval ruler, surrounded by landmarks and the iconic Cathedral.

triumphal.title: Triumphal Arch & Cathedral
triumphal.description: The Arc de Triomphe of Chișinău, built in 1840, stands before the stunning Nativity Cathedral in the central square.

cricova.title: Cricova Winery
cricova.description: An underground wine city spanning 120 km of tunnels, home to one of the world's largest wine collections — including bottles from Hermann Göring's confiscated collection.

orheiul.title: Orheiul Vechi
orheiul.description: A spectacular open-air archaeological complex carved into limestone cliffs, featuring a medieval cave monastery — on UNESCO's tentative list.

cuisine.title: Moldovan Cuisine
cuisine.subtitle: A rich culinary tradition shaped by centuries of cultural crossroads — hearty, flavourful, and best paired with local wine.

mamaliga.title: Mămăligă
mamaliga.description: Traditional cornmeal porridge, Moldova's national dish — served with sour cream, cheese, and stews.

placinte.title: Plăcinte
placinte.description: Thin pastries filled with cheese, potatoes, cabbage, or cherries — a beloved street food and household staple.

wine.title: Moldovan Wine
wine.description: One of the oldest winemaking regions in the world. Try Fetească Neagră, the flagship indigenous red grape variety.

zeama.title: Zeamă
zeama.description: A light, aromatic chicken soup with homemade noodles and lovage — considered the soul of Moldovan comfort food.

sarmale.title: Sarmale
sarmale.description: Cabbage rolls stuffed with a savoury mix of minced meat, rice, and herbs — a festive dish served at every celebration.

coltunasi.title: Colțunași
coltunasi.description: Traditional Moldovan dumplings filled with cottage cheese, potatoes, or cherries — served with sour cream and a touch of nostalgia.
